name: multi-agent-router
description: Capability-based task router: reads an input task description, scores it against a configured routing table of {capability: workflow_id} pairs using keyword matching, and triggers the best-matching sub-workflow. Falls back to a default workflow when no capability matches.

ROUTING_TABLE.description: JSON object mapping capability names to workflow IDs. Each key is a capability name (used for keyword matching against the task) and each value is the workflow_id UUID to dispatch to. Example: '{"data-analysis": "uuid-1", "email-drafting": "uuid-2", "code-review": "uuid-3"}'

DEFAULT_WORKFLOW_ID.description: Workflow ID to dispatch to when no capability matches. If not set and no match is found, the node returns an error.

MATCH_THRESHOLD.description: Minimum number of keyword tokens from the capability name that must appear in the task description for a match (default: 1). Increase for stricter matching.

PASS_THROUGH_INPUT.description: When true, the original task input is forwarded as the sub-workflow's input. When false, only the routing metadata is sent.

output_shape.routed: true if a sub-workflow was selected, false if no match and no default
output_shape.matched_capability: The capability name that matched (null if using default)
output_shape.workflow_id: The workflow_id that was selected for dispatch
output_shape.task_summary: First 200 characters of the input task for traceability
output_shape.all_scores: Object mapping each capability to its match score for debugging

setup_instructions.title: Actor capability ceiling
setup_instructions.detail: The workflow owning actor MUST have max_capability_world: 'automation-node'. Run: grant_capability_ceiling(actor_id: '<actor_id>', world: 'automation-node').

setup_instructions.title: Create sub-workflows first
setup_instructions.detail: Create and publish all sub-workflows that the router will dispatch to. Note the workflow_id UUID of each one.

setup_instructions.title: Configure the routing table
setup_instructions.detail: Set ROUTING_TABLE to a JSON object. Keys should be descriptive, hyphen-separated capability names (these are tokenized for matching). Values are the workflow_id UUIDs. Example: '{"sentiment-analysis": "abc-123", "text-summarization": "def-456"}'.

setup_instructions.title: Input format
setup_instructions.detail: Send input as {"task": "description of what needs to be done"}. The router tokenizes the task and matches tokens against capability name tokens. The best match (highest token overlap) wins.
